title: Authors and Citation

code: @Manual{,   title = {caretMultimodal: Multimodal Late Fusion in R},   author = {Josh Dyce},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/JoshD898/caretMultimodal}, }

title: Multimodal Late Fusion in R
text: caretMultimodal builds caret package make easy train, combine, interpret models multiple datasets modalities. package implements data integration technique called late fusion, base models trained independently dataset, predictions aggregated using ensemble model. work inspired Zach Mayer’s caretEnsemble package, used stacking multiple models single dataset. Late Fusion Workflow

title: Multimodal Late Fusion in R
text: Includes functionality caret, giving users full control sampling strategies, training methods, hyperparameter tuning, Default cross-validation structure careful handling prevent data leakage across modalities Late fusion ensembling using stacked generalization Parallelization faster training across models datasets Model trimming reduce memory usage large ensembles Built-evaluation tools performance assessment, ROC curves, variable importance Detailed error messages simplify debugging

title: Multimodal Late Fusion in R
text: package can installed using devtools
code: devtools::install_github("CompBio-Lab/caretMultimodal")

title: Multimodal Late Fusion in R
text: classification example, use publicly available data sets heart failure, described detail . data included package, can loaded using load_heart_failure(). going use four related datasets (cells, holter, mrna protein) predict whether patients hospitalized 3 months following data collection. example binary classification task (Yes/). use GLMNET method train base ensemble models. resampling, use package default: 5-fold cross-validation repetitions. summary() gives resampling performance metrics base model ensemble using best hyperparameter settings. built functions plot ROC curves --fold predictions, well visualize relative importance base models.
code: set.seed(123L) caretMultimodal::load_heart_failure()  # Set up hyperparameter tuning  alphas <- c(0.7, 0.775, 0.850, 0.925, 1) lambdas <- seq(0.001, 0.1, by = 0.01) tuneGrid <- expand.grid(alpha = alphas, lambda = lambdas)  # Train the base models  heart_models <- caretMultimodal::caret_list(   target = demo$hospitalizations,   data_list = list(     cells = cells,     holter = holter,     mrna = mrna,     proteins = proteins   ),   method = "glmnet",   tuneGrid = tuneGrid )  # Train the ensemble model  heart_stack <- caretMultimodal::caret_stack(     caret_list = heart_models,     method = "glmnet",     tuneGrid = tuneGrid ) summary(heart_stack) #>       model method alpha lambda       ROC      Sens       Spec      ROCSD     SensSD    SpecSD #>      <char> <char> <num>  <num>     <num>     <num>      <num>      <num>      <num>     <num> #> 1:    cells glmnet 0.850  0.091 0.7740741 0.9777778 0.00000000 0.14721931 0.04969040 0.0000000 #> 2:   holter glmnet 0.850  0.081 0.7851852 1.0000000 0.16666667 0.08842471 0.00000000 0.2357023 #> 3:     mrna glmnet 0.925  0.091 0.8111111 0.9777778 0.06666667 0.16789670 0.04969040 0.1490712 #> 4: proteins glmnet 0.700  0.001 0.8962963 0.9555556 0.46666667 0.09128709 0.06085806 0.4472136 #> 5: ensemble glmnet 0.925  0.071 0.9407407 0.9555556 0.40000000 0.06728112 0.09938080 0.2527625 plot_roc(heart_stack) plot_varimp(heart_stack, descending = FALSE)

title: Multimodal Late Fusion in R
text: regression example, use publicly available datasets gestational age, described detail . data included package, can loaded using load_pregnancy(). use seven datasets predict gestational age time blood sampling. , use GLMNET method train base ensemble models. bit preprocessing data remove postpartum samples, use custom trControl implement leave-one-sample-cross-validation. oof_predictions() returns --fold predictions base ensemble models plot_metric() applies custom comparison function put plots results. example, plot shows −log₁₀(p-value) Spearman correlation model’s predictions observed gestational age.
code: set.seed(123L) caretMultimodal::load_gestational_age()  # Remove postpartum samples  postpartum_indices <- which(featureweeks < 0) InputData <- lapply(InputData, function(x) x[-postpartum_indices, ]) featurepatients <- featurepatients[-postpartum_indices] featureweeks <- featureweeks[-postpartum_indices]  # Set up hyperparameter tuning  alphas <- seq(0, 1, 0.1) lambdas <- seq(0, 12, by = 0.5) tuneGrid <- expand.grid(alpha = alphas, lambda = lambdas)  # Set up trControl for leave-one-sample-out cross-validation  loso_folds <- caret::groupKFold(featurepatients, k = length(unique(featurepatients)))  trControl <- caret::trainControl(   method = "cv",   index = loso_folds,   savePredictions = "final",   summaryFunction = caret::defaultSummary )  # Train the base models  pregnancy_models <- caretMultimodal::caret_list(   data_list = InputData,   target = featureweeks,   method = "glmnet",   tuneGrid = tuneGrid,   trControl = trControl )  # Train the ensemble model  pregnancy_stack <- caretMultimodal::caret_stack(   pregnancy_models,   method = "glmnet",   tuneGrid = tuneGrid,   trControl = trControl ) oof_predictions(pregnancy_stack)  #>    Cellfree RNA PlasmaLuminex SerumLuminex Microbiome ImmuneSystem Metabolomics PlasmaSomalogic   ensemble #> 1      14.40391    18.1430558    14.932066  15.869220    16.269368    13.410669       11.833937 13.2816177 #> 2      14.61495    12.7016107    19.211904  12.621349    18.867984    13.998000       13.150318 15.5780514 #> 3      13.59511     7.9551468    14.441999  15.841214    22.322011    12.899592        8.822440 10.1224647 #> 4      15.51295    11.0755650    14.557425  10.926614    10.818393    13.248870        8.705712  8.8447452 #> 5      14.72063    16.7378848     6.824721  15.941069    12.202590    15.744880       11.568229 11.1975881 #>             ...           ...          ...        ...          ...          ...             ...        ... plot_metric(   pregnancy_stack,   metric_function = function(x,y) -log10(cor.test(x,y, method = "spearman")$p.value),   metric_name = "-Log(p-value)" )

title: Create a caret_list object — caret_list
text: Build list caret::train objects, model corresponds data set data_list. resulting list structured use ensembling workflows. Users can specify training method, control parameters, metrics, function supports error handling model trimming efficiency.

title: Create a caret_list object — caret_list
code: caret_list(   target,   data_list,   method,   identifier_column_name = NULL,   trControl = NULL,   metric = NULL,   trim = TRUE,   do_parallel = TRUE,   ... )

title: Create a caret_list object — caret_list
text: target Target parameter vector, either numeric regression factor/character classification. data_list list data sets train models method method train models . Can custom method one found caret::modelLookup(). identifier_column_name name column connects rows dataset (ex. participant ID). provided, column must present datasets within data_list proper matching. Use datasets different numbers rows. Currently, providing identifier_column_name disables support custom trControl, new resampling scheme must constructed dataset row matching, may invalidate user-supplied fold indices. trControl Control use caret::train function. NULL, default control constructed depending target type. metric Metric use caret::train function. NULL, default metric constructed depending target type. trim Logical, whether train models trimmed save memory. Default TRUE do_parallel Logical, whether parallelize model training across datasets. Default TRUE ... Additional arguments pass caret::train function

title: Create a caret_list object — caret_list
text: caret_list object, list caret::train model corresponding data_list.

what: Ensemble the models of a caret_list object — caret_stack
title: Ensemble the models of a caret_list object — caret_stack
text: Train ensemble (stacked) model base learners caret_list. ensemble caret::train model learns combine predictions base models. default, meta-learner trained --fold predictions resampling process, ensuring ensemble overfit -sample predictions. Alternatively, new datasets can supplied via data_list target transfer-learning style ensembling.

title: Ensemble the models of a caret_list object — caret_stack
code: caret_stack(   caret_list,   method,   data_list = NULL,   target = NULL,   metric = NULL,   trControl = NULL,   ... )

title: Ensemble the models of a caret_list object — caret_stack
text: caret_list caret_list object method method train ensemble model. Can custom method one found caret::modelLookup(). data_list list datasets predict , dataset matching corresponding model caret_list. NULL, --fold predictions base models used. target Target parameter vector must provided predicting new data list. NULL, target vector used train base models used. metric Metric use caret::train function. NULL, default metric constructed depending target type. trControl Control use caret::train function. NULL, default control constructed depending target type. TODO hyperlink default arguments + add quick description ... Additional arguments pass caret::train

title: Ensemble the models of a caret_list object — caret_stack
text: caret_stack object.

what: Perform an ablation analysis for a caret_stack model. — compute_ablation.caret_stack
title: Perform an ablation analysis for a caret_stack model. — compute_ablation.caret_stack
text: function performs systematic ablation analysis caret_stack ensemble evaluate base learner's contribution predictive performance. procedure begins full set base model predictions iteratively removes one model time. iteration, ensemble model retrained remaining learners using method, tuneGrid, trControl settings original stack. Model importance ensemble estimated, learner lowest (highest, reverse = TRUE) relative contribution removed. ablation step, --fold predictions generated evaluated using user-supplied metric_function, accept two arguments (predictions, target) return single numeric value (e.g., RMSE, accuracy, AUC). resulting performance metrics model contribution estimates recorded stage returned data.table.

title: Perform an ablation analysis for a caret_stack model. — compute_ablation.caret_stack
code: # S3 method for class 'caret_stack' compute_ablation(object, metric_function, metric_name, reverse = FALSE, ...)

title: Perform an ablation analysis for a caret_stack model. — compute_ablation.caret_stack
text: object caret_stack object. metric_function function takes two arguments (predictions, target) returns single numeric value representing metric compute (e.g., RMSE, accuracy, AUC). metric_name name metric reverse direction ablate . FALSE, lowest contributing model removed iteration. TRUE, highest contributing model removed. Default FALSE. ... used. Included S3 compatibility.

title: Perform an ablation analysis for a caret_stack model. — compute_ablation.caret_stack
text: data.table

what: Compute the feature level contributions for a caret_stack model. — compute_feature_contributions.caret_stack
title: Compute the feature level contributions for a caret_stack model. — compute_feature_contributions.caret_stack
text: Feature-level contributions ensemble model computed using two-stage application caret::varImp. First, varImp applied ensemble model, base-model predictions treated features, yielding dataset-level weights. Next, varImp applied base model obtain feature-level importance scores within dataset. final contribution individual feature ensemble calculated product dataset-level weight feature-level importance within corresponding base model.

title: Compute the feature level contributions for a caret_stack model. — compute_feature_contributions.caret_stack
code: # S3 method for class 'caret_stack' compute_feature_contributions(object, n_features = 20, ...)

title: Compute the feature level contributions for a caret_stack model. — compute_feature_contributions.caret_stack
text: object caret_stack object. n_features maximum number features include. Setting large value include features. Default 20. ... used. Included S3 compatibility.

title: Out-of-fold predictions from a caret_stack — oof_predictions.caret_stack
text: Retrieve --fold predictions corresponding best hyperparameter setting trained ensemble model. predictions come resampling process (final refit) can optionally aggregated across resamples produce single prediction per training instance. base model predictions returned training data ensemble; depending model setup, may true --fold predictions simply fitted values. classification models, predictions always exclude first class index.
